title: Plasmid Extraction (Plasmid Isolation)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"82ten","text":"2023 NUS-Singapore iGEM team followed this protocol to isolate the plasmid from the cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"69rjk","text":"Glycerol stock (  of cell stock in   of 100% glycerol solution) may be prepared and keep it in the   fridge before the plasmid extraction procedure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":16,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":35,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":99,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"4uu7o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"900","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}},"1":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"300","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}},"2":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"-80","unit":10}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"31ugs","text":"Proper lab PPE must be worn at all times.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Incubate the cells containing the plasmid of interest in a Falcon tube with 5 mL of LB media and 5 µL of the appropriate antibiotics Overnight at 37 °C before starting the plasmid extraction.
Take out the Falcon tube with the cultured cells from the incubator.
Centrifuge the Falcon tube at 5000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Discard the supernatant in the Falcon tube and keep the cell pellet.
Add 250 µL of Buffer P1 Qiagen Catalog #19051 (with RNase A Qiagen Catalog #19101 added, kept in the 4 °C fridge) into the Falcon tube and resuspend the cell pellet.
Transfer the whole solution into a Eppendorf tube.
Add 250 µL of Buffer P2 Qiagen Catalog #19052 into the Eppendorf tube to lyse the cells.
Shake the Eppendorf tube for mixing.
Add 350μL of Buffer N3 Qiagen Catalog #19064 into the Eppendorf tube.
Shake the Eppendorf tube for mixing, a cloudy solution shall be observed.
Centrifuge the Eppendorf tube at 13 rpm, °C, 10 minutes .
Transfer the supernatant into a Mini Prep tube and discard the cell debris.
Centrifuge it at 13 rpm, , 1 minute .
Discard the flow-through and place the Mini Prep tube back into the same tube.
Add 500 µL of PB buffer Qiagen Catalog #19066 into the Mini Prep tube.
Discard the flow-through and place the Mini Prep tube back into the same tube.
Add 700 µL of Buffer PE Qiagen Catalog #19065 into the Mini Prep tube.
Discard the flow-through and place the Mini Prep tube back into the same tube.
Transfer the Mini Prep column into the newly labelled Eppendorf tube.
Add 50 µL of DI water into the Eppendorf tube.
Discard the Mini Prep column, the solution left in the Eppendorf tube is the plasmid.

Use NanoDrop NanoDrop™ One/OneC Microvolume UV-Vis Spectrophotometer (UV-Vis Spectrophotometer, Thermo Scientific, ND-ONE-W) to measure the concentration (under "dsDNA" mode) and the purity of the plasmid.
Store the isolated plasmid at room temperature.
Centrifuge it at 13 rpm, , 1 minute .
Centrifuge it at 13 rpm, , 1 minute .
Centrifuge it at 13 rpm, , 1 minute to remove the Buffer PE Qiagen Catalog #19065 residual.
Centrifuge it at 13 rpm, , 1 minute , ensuring that the direction of the Eppendorf tube’s cap is the same as the direction of spinning to avoid breaking.
creator: NUS iGEM
